consultation.chiefComplaint: Excessive daytime sleepiness for 6 months, loud snoring reported by spouse, morning headaches 3-4 times per week
consultation.historyOfPresentIllness: 42-year-old male presents with progressive excessive daytime sleepiness (EDS) over the past 6 months. Epworth Sleepiness Scale score 16/24 (pathological). Spouse reports loud snoring with witnessed apneic episodes occurring 4-5 times per night. Patient reports unrefreshing sleep despite 7-8 hours in bed. Morning headaches 3-4 times per week. No history of narcolepsy symptoms (no cataplexy, sleep paralysis, or hypnagogic hallucinations). Denies alcohol use. Caffeine intake: 3-4 cups/day.
consultation.pastMedicalHistory: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus diagnosed 2019, on Metformin 500mg BD. Hypertension diagnosed 2020, on Telmisartan 40mg OD. No surgical history. No prior sleep studies. Family history: Father had OSA (diagnosed at age 55). Mother: Type 2 DM.
consultation.physicalExamination: General: Obese male, no acute distress. ENT: Mallampati Grade III (high risk for OSA), neck circumference 42cm (>40cm threshold for OSA risk), retrognathia noted, no tonsillar hypertrophy (Grade 1). Chest: Clear bilaterally, no wheeze/crepitations. CVS: S1S2 normal, no murmurs. Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, obese. CNS: Alert, oriented, no focal neurological deficits.
consultation.notes: Patient is a software engineer with irregular work hours. Driving 45 minutes to office daily — critical to assess driving safety with EDS.
